reasoning: The baseline established by 'A Minecraft Movie' is a massive $162.7 million domestic opening weekend box office (set in April 2025). This is currently the largest opening of 2025. However, the remainder of 2025 has several heavy-hitting blockbusters, most notably Superman (July 11), which is tracking for an opening of $154M to $175M+. Other significant threats include Jurassic World Rebirth (July 2), Zootopia 2 (November), and Avatar: Fire and Ash (December). Given the strong long-range tracking on Superman specifically, combined with the historical precedent of massive summer and winter weekend openings (Jurassic World, Inside Out 2, etc.), there is a solid probability that at least one of these upcoming releases eclipses $162.7M. The Polymarket consensus on May 29 was 43%, which perfectly accounts for this known Minecraft threshold versus the upcoming 2025 slate. Factoring in recent strong tracking for Superman, I estimate a roughly 41% chance that Minecraft maintains its number 1 spot through the end of the year.
